proposals.title: Large-magnitude double accumulation → last-digit mismatch in SEC-EDGAR financial values
proposals.detect: validation fails with ±0.01 difference in values ≥ 10^13; value.bin column type is double; output format %.2f; mismatch rows are the highest-value entities. Kahan summation does NOT fix this — the values simply exceed double's 15.95-digit capacity.
proposals.fix: Accumulate using int64_t with implicit cents scale: read double v → int64_t iv = llround(v * 100.0); accumulate iv; output as printf("%" PRId64 ".%02" PRId64, sum/100, abs(sum%100)). This preserves exact integer arithmetic regardless of magnitude.

proposals.title: Massive PRE hash table (>10M rows → 2^25 slots, ~600MB) causes build_joins to dominate at 19–65s
proposals.detect: build_joins > 10s with a hash table CAP ≥ 2^24; table footprint (CAP × sizeof(Slot)) > 256MB; parallel CAS build still slow because LLC capacity is 32-64MB and table is 10x larger → every probe misses cache.
proposals.fix: Apply P11 (pre-built mmap hash index) for the PRE table. If no pre-built index exists, partition the build: split pre_n rows into K=4 hash-prefix buckets fitting in L3; build each bucket sequentially with different threads. Alternatively, use a Robin Hood hash with tombstone instead of CAS — reduces probe distance and false-sharing.

proposals.title: P17 (thread-local aggregation_merge) should be applied by default — always yields 10-100x merge reduction
proposals.detect: aggregation_merge > 20ms in iter_0. Seen Q1 (49ms→2ms) and Q6 (320ms→3ms) both in this run. Universal win with no correctness risk.
proposals.fix: In code generation, always use thread-local AggSlot arrays merged into global map post-barrier. Never use a single shared map with atomics for GROUP BY aggregation.
